key_drivers: The Federal Reserve remains on hold, while intraday dollar firmness and still-restrictive U.S. policy expectations continue to weigh on metals, equity beta, and non-USD FX.
The latest EIA data showed a crude inventory draw and geopolitical tension remains elevated, keeping the oil backdrop more supportive than the rest of the cross-asset complex.
China's March PMI rebound and the RBA's March hike are helping AUD outperform the broader FX basket, even as real yields stay broadly flat and credit conditions are no longer improving.

fallbacks_used: Federal Reserve calendar materials were used as the official Fed stance fallback because CME FedWatch did not expose the probability table cleanly.
The accessible World Gold Council March 2026 ETF-flows research note was used because the ETF data page required sign-in for the detailed flow charts.
